factors.name: Bond Selloff & Real Yields
factors.summary: Bloomberg reported 'Bond Selloff Threatens to Knock AI Stock Frenzy Off Course' (May 16, 13:31); Warsh commentary notes inflation spiking with Treasury yields surging, creating headwinds for long-duration tech multiples despite AI enthusiasm.

factors.name: CPI/Inflation Surprise (April)
factors.summary: MarketWatch headline (May 16, 17:38): 'April's inflation spike leaves Warsh and the Fed zero excuses not to raise rates.' Inflation persistence threatens multiple compression for NDX; bond markets pricing in hawkish Fed repricing.

factors.name: AI Capex & Hyperscaler Momentum
factors.summary: BlackRock weighing $5–10B SpaceX IPO investment; Cerebras blockbuster IPO boosting AI hype; retail AI ETF inflows record-breaking six-week run. Tech CEO participation in Trump-Xi Beijing summit signals continued confidence in tech/AI policy environment.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: No mega-cap earnings releases in past 24h window; Bill Ackman built Microsoft stake on AI/cloud bet. Forward guidance dry until next earnings season; sentiment data-dependent.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran, Taiwan, Ebola)
factors.summary: WHO declared Congo Ebola outbreak a global health emergency; US-Israel preparing potential Iran military ops renewal; Trump-Xi Taiwan commitments remain ambiguous. These tail risks elevate volatility and commodity pressure (oil concerns offset some AI euphoria).

factors.name: Dollar & USD Strength
factors.summary: India tightening silver imports to defend rupee (currency pressure); no major DXY catalyst in news. Tech earnings heavily weighted to international revenue; strong USD headwind not yet dominant but nascent currency stress noted.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation & Risk-Off Signals
factors.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETFs shed $1B in single week, snapping six-week inflow run; crypto longs lost $500M as BTC slid to $78K. Capital rotation from risk assets (BTC, crypto) toward 'safety' contradicts strong equity enthusiasm; NQ-BTC decoupling suggests sentiment fragility.

factors.name: Semiconductor & Supply Chain
factors.summary: SK Hynix set to become 2nd Korean company to hit $1T market cap; no major TSMC/ASML disruptions. Positive for chip demand outlook but no fresh catalyst in 24h window.

factors.name: Labor & Macro Stress
factors.summary: Long Island Rail Road strike (first in 30+ years, stranding 300K daily riders); 'quarter of recent layoffs attributed to AI' per May 17 headline. Labor cost inflation and AI displacement narrative weigh on broader consumer confidence and wage growth narrative.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations (Warsh Era Begins)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh entering Fed facing 'family fight' over rate cuts; inflation spiking and yields surging signal FOMC in no mood to ease. Rate pause/hike bias contradicts growth narrative; 4-6 week Fed hold likely.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between competing forces: AI capex euphoria and mega-cap tech positioning on one side (Cerebras IPO, BlackRock SpaceX bet, retail ETF record inflows) versus rising real yields, inflation surprises, and geopolitical tail risks (Iran, Taiwan, Ebola) on the other. The bond selloff and April CPI spike are forcing Fed repricing—Warsh commentary signals higher-for-longer rates, directly hostile to long-duration growth multiples. Crypto capital rotation out of BTC (−$1B ETF outflows) and labor disruptions (LIRR strike) add micro stress. The 24h decline of −0.22% and 4h weakness of −0.67% suggest a Phase 2 pullback from earlier AI enthusiasm, with no clear retest catalyst imminent.
catalysts_ahead: Fed speakers / Warsh confirmation hearing updates (next 1–2 weeks)
CPI print expected late May (inflation trend critical for rate path)
NVIDIA earnings (likely June; AI demand commentary will be NDX driver)
Tesla, Microsoft, Google earnings (May–June window)
Treasury auction and yield curve reaction (ongoing, daily impact)
China geopolitical developments (Taiwan arms sales decision, Putin Beijing visit May 19–20)
Ebola outbreak containment updates (systemic risk if spreads; energy & supply chain implications)
screening.risk_warning: This bias would be invalidated if (1) CPI surprise comes in cooler-than-expected, repricing Fed cuts back into market and crushing yields; (2) major hyperscaler (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) guidance smashes expectations, reigniting AI mega-cap rally and justifying multiples; (3) geopolitical tensions ease abruptly (Iran deal, Taiwan truce); or (4) Fed pivots dovish on data softness. Current weakness is NOT yet a reversal—watch for NDX retest of 29,300+ to confirm Phase 3 extension or pullback-support stability.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is early Phase 2 (−0.67% 4h, shallow), not yet extended; avoid chasing shorts into support 29,000. Wait for retest of 29,300 resistance or clear NDX close below 28,900 to define next leg. If 10Y yield breaks above 4.35%, bearish confirmation likely; if breaks below 4.20%, watch for bullish reversal into AI names. No high-conviction entry until macro (CPI, Fed speakers) clarifies rate path.

factors.summary: SK Hynix set to hit $1T market cap; 'AI boom fuels record memory-chip profits' despite cyclical risks noted. Samsung labor strike in South Korea being managed diplomatically. Semiconductor demand outlook remains strong.

factors.summary: Kevin Warsh joining Fed amid hawkish consensus; 'bond markets won't wait for Fed to combat inflation' per MarketWatch. Rate-cut expectations appear fully priced out; any new Fed speaker could re-anchor rates higher.

factors.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETFs bled $1B in a week (ending six-week inflow run); capital rotating toward AI but also acknowledging yield threats. Retail enthusiasm persists (Cerebras retail euphoria) but not overwhelmingly bullish.

factors.summary: Bitcoin below $78K, SOL and XRP down ~5%, crypto longs lost $500M. BTC weakness historically signals broader risk-off tone; capital fleeing to safety and shorter-duration assets.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between two conflicting narratives: powerful AI capex momentum and hyperscaler demand (SpaceX, Cerebras, semiconductor strength) versus aggressive bond-market re-pricing and inflation concerns that threaten multiple compression on long-duration growth stocks. Recent 4h change of -0.67% and 24h decline of -0.22% suggest mild consolidation after a multi-week rally, with Bitcoin weakness and ETF outflows (crypto bleeds $1B) confirming risk-off undertones. Kevin Warsh's Fed appointment and rising real yields (Treasury selloff ongoing) pose the largest near-term headwind; rate expectations are being reset higher by bond markets, not lower.

Fed speakers including Kevin Warsh (new member, hawkish positioning)

screening.risk_warning: A larger-than-expected inflation surprise OR hawkish Fed signal this week would likely trigger sharp 1–2% selloff in NQ. Conversely, a dovish inflation read (surprising on the low side) or Fed pivot dovish would reverse bias to BULLISH instantly. Geopolitical de-escalation (Strait of Hormuz reopens, Iran war thaws) could also spark relief rally. Additionally, mega-cap earnings beats with strong AI capex guidance could override rate concerns and fuel BULLISH reversal. Monitor BTC and crypto for capitulation signals that would suggest capitulation bottom forming.
screening.entry_guidance: Current price action is in early consolidation phase (small 4h loss, modest 24h decline). Avoid chasing lower; wait for either a test of support 28,900–29,000 with bullish reversal candle (if inflation data cools or Fed signals patience) OR a break above 29,500 on AI capex news. Immediate entry risk/reward is poor; clarity from CPI or Fed speaker needed before committed directional move.
